name: Paxos
def: The foundational consensus protocol for agreeing a single value among unreliable nodes, tolerating crashes via majority quorums.
problem: Reach agreement — and, via Multi-Paxos, replicate an ordered log — in an asynchronous network where nodes and messages can fail.
mechanism: Proposers run a two-phase prepare/accept protocol with numbered proposals; a value is chosen once a majority accepts it. Multi-Paxos elects a stable leader to skip phase 1 per entry.
benefits: Proven safe under asynchrony and crash faults
The theoretical basis of most replication systems
limits: Famously hard to understand and implement correctly
Bare Paxos agrees one value — Multi-Paxos adds real complexity
Duelling proposers can livelock without a leader
failures: Subtle bugs from the under-specified original
whenNot: When Raft’s understandability is worth more than pedigree
myths: "Paxos and Raft solve different problems" — both do log replication; Raft just restructures Paxos for clarity

complexity.Note: 2 message rounds to choose; commit needs a majority quorum
